title: Szydbb1L2Ue5B57Wyio35Mac5Mcavu
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) earlier this month released its latest grain export forecast for the 2024/25 season and is now predicting that global coarse grain, wheat, soybean, and soymeal exports will total 702.3 million tons. This is 1.8 million tons less than was predicted a month ago and would mark a year-on-year decline of 10.1 million tons (-1%) from the 712.4 million tons that is now expected for 2023/24. Global grain trade being expected to contract on a year-on-year basis remains a headwind, but at least a smaller contraction is now expected.
